overview.title: AXTP Protocol
overview.summary: AXTP is a transport-independent device communication protocol for CONTROL, RPC and STREAM payloads across TCP, WebSocket, USB HID, BLE and UART transports.
goals: Provide one unified protocol model for control, request/response RPC and stream transfer.
Keep production clients small by binding each transport to a fixed frame profile.
Support both standard and compact binary frame paths.
Keep full dynamic capability modeling optional outside AXTP v1 Core.
nonGoals: Full dynamic UI capability modeling is not required in v1.
WebSocket Text and HTTP JSON are debug or legacy adapter paths.
STREAM is not carried over WebSocket Text or HTTP JSON in production.
Header profile negotiation is not performed dynamically in v1.

layers.name: Transport Profile
layers.description: Defines how AXTP runs over TCP, WebSocket, HID, BLE or UART.

layers.name: Frame Profile
layers.description: Binds L1 Frame Header and L2 Payload Header into one parser profile.

layers.name: Payload Layer
layers.description: Carries CONTROL, RPC or STREAM payloads.

layers.name: RPC Session
layers.description: Handles Hello, Identify, requests, responses, events and errors.

layers.name: Stream Session
layers.description: Handles OTA, file, audio, video, log and sensor chunk transfer.

lifecycle.description: Open an AXTP logical session and declare runtime limits.
Accept the session and return final runtime parameters.
Announce RPC session rules, protocol version and authentication requirements.
Submit client identity and optional authentication data.
Confirm that the RPC session is ready.
Query the method bitmap available to the current session.

optionalLifecycleExtensions.description: Reserved for transports that need an explicit client acknowledgement after ACCEPT; not required by AXTP v1 Core.

quickStart.title: Minimal Client Startup
steps: Connect using a Transport Profile.
Send CONTROL OPEN.
Wait for CONTROL ACCEPT.
Wait for Hello.
Send Identify if required.
Query capability.supportedMethods.
Start RPC requests.

quickStart.title: Cloud Reverse Connection
steps: Role mapping: Device = Physical Client + Logical Server. Cloud = Physical Server + Logical Client.
Device initiates WebSocket connection to cloud endpoint.
Device sends CONTROL OPEN (device is Physical Client but drives session setup).
Cloud sends CONTROL ACCEPT.
Device sends RPC Hello (op=0) — device is Logical Server, so device always sends Hello.
Cloud sends RPC Identify (op=2).
Device sends RPC Identified (op=3) with session ID.
Cloud sends capability.supportedMethods request to discover device capabilities.
Cloud issues RPC requests to device as normal.

quickStart.title: DS-RPC Debug Adapter
steps: Purpose: browser or curl debugging only. Not for production. No STREAM support.
Connect via WebSocket Text (ws://device/axtp-debug or similar endpoint).
No CONTROL OPEN/ACCEPT handshake — connection enters FRAMING_READY directly.
Device sends DS-RPC Hello: {"sid":"","op":0,"d":{"rpcVersion":"..."}}
Client sends DS-RPC Identify: {"sid":"","op":2,"d":{"clientName":"..."}}
Device sends DS-RPC Identified: {"sid":"<uuid>","op":3,"d":{"negotiatedRpcVersion":"..."}}
Issue RPC calls as JSON: {"sid":"<uuid>","op":7,"d":{"id":"00000001","method":"display.setBrightness","params":{"value":80}}}
Receive response: {"sid":"<uuid>","op":8,"d":{"id":"00000001","status":{"ok":true,"code":0}}}

quickStart.title: Compact Fallback
steps: Use Compact Frame only when transport MTU <= 64B (HID-64, BLE ATT, UART fixed packet).
Use Standard Frame for USB HID with Report Size > 64B (define AXTP-HID-HS Transport Profile).
Compact limitations: no Magic field, CRC8 only, max 15 fragments, no SourceId/DestinationId.
Frame Profile is fixed for the entire session — no runtime switching.
If Standard is needed later, close the session and reconnect with a Standard-capable transport.

transports.name: AXTP-WS
transports.notes: One AXTP Frame per WebSocket Binary Message. No extra framing needed.

transports.name: AXTP-TCP
transports.notes: Byte-stream transport. Receiver must reassemble frames using Magic+Length.

transports.name: AXTP-HID-HS
transports.notes: USB HID with Report Size > 64B. Specific report size is implementation-defined.

transports.name: AXTP-HID-64
transports.notes: USB HID with 64B Report. 58B usable payload after 4B header and 1B CRC8 and 1B ReportID.

transports.name: AXTP-BLE-RPC
transports.notes: BLE GATT. MTU negotiated at BLE layer; typical range 20B-247B.

transports.name: AXTP-UART
transports.notes: UART with COBS or SLIP framing at transport layer.

transports.name: AXTP-WS-CLOUD-REVERSE
transports.notes: Device is Physical Client but Logical Server. Device sends Hello after ACCEPT.

transports.name: AXTP-WS-TEXT
transports.notes: DS-RPC JSON mode. No AXTP Frame Header. No STREAM support. Debug only.

payloadTypes.name: CONTROL
payloadTypes.selectionRule: Use for session lifecycle signals — OPEN, ACCEPT, HEARTBEAT, ACK, NACK, CLOSE, RESUME. Never use for business data.

payloadTypes.name: RPC
payloadTypes.description: Binary request, response, event and error payload.
payloadTypes.selectionRule: Use for all business operations — method calls, responses, events, and batch. Choose JSON or BINARY encoding per capability.

headerFields.name: requestId
headerFields.description: Client-assigned request correlation ID. Little-Endian. 0 for events.

headerFields.name: methodOrEventId
headerFields.description: methodId for Request/Response; eventId for Event. Little-Endian.

headerFields.name: bodyEncoding
headerFields.description: Body encoding: 0x00=NONE, 0x01=TLV8, 0x02=TLV16. Only meaningful when rpcEncoding=BINARY.

payloadTypes.name: STREAM
payloadTypes.selectionRule: Use for high-throughput data — OTA firmware chunks, video/audio frames, file transfer, log streaming. Never carry business metadata in STREAM; use RPC for setup and teardown.

headerFields.name: streamId
headerFields.description: Stream identifier assigned by firmware.begin or stream.open response. Little-Endian.

headerFields.name: seqId
headerFields.description: Monotonically increasing chunk sequence number. Starts at 0. Little-Endian.

headerFields.name: cursor
headerFields.description: Byte offset of this chunk within the stream. Used for resume and integrity checks. Little-Endian.

rules: v1 Core requires OPEN and ACCEPT before RPC or STREAM payloads.
READY is not required by v1 Core and must not be assumed by peers.
Dynamic header negotiation is not performed in v1.
STREAM uses the unified 16-byte header in production transports.
The historical 8-byte stream header is not part of AXTP v1 Core.
WebSocket Text and HTTP JSON must not carry production STREAM data.
Stable methodId, eventId and error code values must not be reused.
Legacy command mappings may be preserved as metadata under methods[].legacy.
WebSocket Text and HTTP JSON may exist only as debug or legacy adapter paths.

fields.name: imageType
fields.description: Firmware image type.

fields.name: imageSize
fields.description: Full firmware image size in bytes.

fields.name: imageVersion
fields.description: Target firmware version.

fields.name: verifyType
fields.description: Object-level verification algorithm such as md5, crc32 or sha256.

fields.name: verifyValue
fields.description: Hex verification value for the full firmware image.

fields.name: chunkSizeHint
fields.description: Preferred STREAM chunk size in bytes.

fields.name: windowSizeHint
fields.description: Preferred stream-layer send window.

fields.name: flags
fields.description: OTA behavior flags such as resume or force-update hints.

fields.name: transferId
fields.description: OTA transfer identifier.

fields.name: streamId
fields.description: STREAM data channel identifier.

fields.name: acceptedOffset
fields.description: First byte offset the sender should transmit.

fields.name: chunkSize
fields.description: Negotiated STREAM chunk size in bytes.

fields.name: windowSize
fields.description: Negotiated stream-layer send window.

fields.name: resumeToken
fields.description: Opaque resume token.

fields.name: otaState
fields.description: Current OTA state.

fields.name: profile
fields.description: Bound Stream Profile, usually firmware.ota.

fields.name: ackMode
fields.description: Stream-layer reliability mode.

fields.name: cursorUnit
fields.description: Cursor unit used by STREAM packets, usually byteOffset.

fields.name: maxDataSize
fields.description: Maximum STREAM data bytes per packet.

fields.name: transferId
fields.description: OTA transfer identifier.

fields.name: totalBytesSent
fields.description: Total firmware bytes sent by the host.

fields.name: totalChunks
fields.description: Total STREAM chunks sent by the host.

fields.name: receivedBytes
fields.description: Total bytes accepted by the device.

fields.name: receivedChunks
fields.description: Total chunks accepted by the device.

fields.name: otaState
fields.description: Current OTA state.

fields.name: transferId
fields.description: OTA transfer identifier.

fields.name: verifyResult
fields.description: Verification result for the transferred image.

fields.name: otaState
fields.description: Current OTA state.

fields.name: transferId
fields.description: OTA transfer identifier.

fields.name: applyMode
fields.description: Apply mode such as APPLY_NOW, APPLY_ON_REBOOT or STAGE_ONLY.

fields.name: otaState
fields.description: Current OTA state.

fields.name: rebootRequired
fields.description: Whether applying the image requires reboot.

fields.name: transportProfile
fields.description: Supported HID Transport Profile, usually AXTP-HID-64.

fields.name: maxStreamCount
fields.description: Maximum simultaneously opened media streams.

fields.name: maxChunkSize
fields.description: Maximum STREAM data bytes per HID-64 report after the 16B STREAM header.

fields.name: supportsVideo
fields.description: Device can open media.video streams over HID.

fields.name: supportsAudio
fields.description: Device can open media.audio streams over HID.

schemas.name: StreamOpenRequest
schemas.description: Parameters for opening a HID-backed media STREAM channel.

fields.name: profile
fields.description: Stream Profile name. For this method use media.video or media.audio.

fields.name: transportProfile
fields.description: Transport Profile name. HID media streams use AXTP-HID-64.

fields.name: direction
fields.description: Stream direction; values are device_to_host, host_to_device, or bidirectional.

fields.name: mediaKind
fields.description: Media kind carried by this stream; values are video or audio.

fields.name: sourceId
fields.description: Device-local media source identifier.

fields.name: codec
fields.description: Codec or sample format, for example h264, mjpeg, pcm_s16le, or opus.

fields.name: width
fields.description: Video width in pixels. Required for video streams when not implied by sourceId.

fields.name: height
fields.description: Video height in pixels. Required for video streams when not implied by sourceId.

fields.name: frameRate
fields.description: Video frame rate in frames per second.

fields.name: sampleRate
fields.description: Audio sample rate in Hz.

fields.name: channelCount
fields.description: Number of audio channels.

fields.name: chunkSizeHint
fields.description: Preferred STREAM data bytes per HID-64 report after the 16B STREAM header.

fields.name: windowSizeHint
fields.description: Preferred stream flow-control window in chunks.

schemas.name: StreamOpenResponse
schemas.description: Result of a successful STREAM media channel open.

fields.name: streamId
fields.description: STREAM channel identifier bound to the selected Stream Profile.

fields.name: profile
fields.description: Accepted Stream Profile name.

fields.name: transportProfile
fields.description: Accepted Transport Profile name.

fields.name: chunkSize
fields.description: STREAM data bytes to place in each HID-64 report after the 16B STREAM header.

fields.name: windowSize
fields.description: Negotiated stream flow-control window in chunks.

fields.name: ackMode
fields.description: Stream acknowledgement mode, such as best_effort or stop_and_wait.

fields.name: cursorUnit
fields.description: Cursor unit used in the 64-bit STREAM cursor, usually timestampUs for media streams.

schemas.name: StreamOpenedEvent
schemas.description: Payload emitted when a STREAM media channel is opened.

fields.name: streamId
fields.description: Opened STREAM channel identifier.

fields.name: profile
fields.description: Bound Stream Profile name.

fields.name: transportProfile
fields.description: Bound Transport Profile name.

fields.name: mediaKind
fields.description: Media kind carried by the stream; values are video or audio.

schemas.name: StreamErrorEvent
schemas.description: Payload emitted when a STREAM media channel fails.

fields.name: streamId
fields.description: STREAM channel identifier, absent if the failure happened before allocation.

fields.name: errorCode
fields.description: AXTP ErrorCode value describing the stream failure.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Optional diagnostic text.

wireExamples.title: USB HID High Speed — Full Session Establishment
wireExamples.description: Complete session establishment over USB HID with Report Size > 64B. Standard Frame Profile (12B L1 Header + CRC16). All integers Little-Endian.

steps.label: 2. CONTROL ACCEPT
steps.asciiLayout:
Standard L1 Header (12B) + Control Payload (17B) + CRC16 (2B)
opcode=0x02 (ACCEPT), controlId=1, statusCode=0x0000
TLV body: sessionId=0x00000001, protocolVersion=1, maxFrameSize=512, mtu=512

steps.label: 3. RPC Hello (JSON)
steps.asciiLayout:
Standard L1 Header (12B) + RPC Binary Header (11B) + JSON body + CRC16 (2B)
rpcEncoding=0x01 (JSON), rpcOp=0x00 (Hello), requestId=0, methodOrEventId=0

steps.label: 4. RPC Identify (JSON)
steps.asciiLayout:
Standard L1 Header (12B) + RPC Binary Header (11B) + JSON body + CRC16 (2B)
rpcEncoding=0x01 (JSON), rpcOp=0x02 (Identify), requestId=1

steps.label: 5. RPC Identified (JSON)
steps.asciiLayout:
Standard L1 Header (12B) + RPC Binary Header (11B) + JSON body + CRC16 (2B)
rpcEncoding=0x01 (JSON), rpcOp=0x03 (Identified), requestId=1

steps.label: 6. capability.supportedMethods Request (Binary TLV)
steps.asciiLayout:
Standard L1 Header (12B) + RPC Binary Header (11B) + TLV body (empty) + CRC16 (2B)
rpcEncoding=0x02 (BINARY), rpcOp=0x07 (Request), methodId=0x0301

steps.label: 7. capability.supportedMethods Response (Binary TLV)
steps.asciiLayout:
Standard L1 Header (12B) + RPC Binary Header (11B) + TLV body + CRC16 (2B)
rpcOp=0x08 (RequestResponse), methodId=0x0301
TLV body: methodMasks field containing per-domain bitmasks
fieldAnnotations: TLV methodMasks example: 01 04 05 01 00 00 = fieldId=0x01 len=4 value=[DomainId=0x05 MaskLen=0x01 Bitmask=0x03] meaning display domain supports getBrightness(bit0) and setBrightness(bit1)

methods.name: device.getInfo
methods.description: Return static device identity and firmware metadata.

methods.name: capability.supportedMethods
methods.description: Return the per-domain method bitmap supported by the current session.

methods.name: display.getBrightness
methods.description: Read the current display brightness percentage.

methods.name: display.setBrightness
methods.description: Set the display brightness and optionally request a transition duration.

methods.name: firmware.begin
methods.description: Begin a firmware OTA transfer and allocate the STREAM context.

methods.name: firmware.end
methods.description: Finish sending firmware data for the active OTA stream.

methods.name: firmware.verify
methods.description: Verify the transferred firmware object before applying it.

methods.name: stream.open
methods.description: Open an AXTP STREAM media channel over HID using media.video or media.audio, without UVC or UAC.

events.name: firmware.updateProgress
events.description: Emitted while firmware OTA transfer or processing advances.

events.name: firmware.updateCompleted
events.description: Emitted when firmware update processing completes successfully.

events.name: firmware.updateFailed
events.description: Emitted when firmware update processing fails.

events.name: stream.opened
events.description: Emitted after a STREAM media channel is opened and bound to a streamId.

events.name: stream.error
events.description: Emitted when a STREAM media channel fails or cannot continue.

errors.name: FRAME_FRAGMENT_MISSING
errors.message: One or more frame fragments are missing.

errors.name: CONTROL_OPEN_REQUIRED
errors.message: Session has not completed CONTROL OPEN.

errors.name: RESERVED_CONTROL_PROFILE_UNSUPPORTED
errors.message: Historical header profile negotiation error. AXTP v1 implementations must not emit it.

errors.name: RPC_METHOD_NOT_FOUND
errors.message: MethodId or method name is not supported.

errors.name: MEDIA_SOURCE_NOT_FOUND
errors.message: Requested media source does not exist.

errors.name: MEDIA_SOURCE_UNAVAILABLE
errors.message: Requested media source is currently unavailable.

errors.name: MEDIA_CODEC_UNSUPPORTED
errors.message: Requested media codec or sample format is unsupported.

errors.name: MEDIA_FRAMERATE_UNSUPPORTED
errors.message: Requested video frame rate is unsupported.

errors.name: MEDIA_STREAM_START_FAILED
errors.message: Device failed to start the requested media stream.
